key_insight: The paper extends FFT-based mechanical solvers to handle both periodic and non-periodic boundary conditions in parallel, using discrete trigonometric transforms and tailored Green operators for robust linear and nonlinear analysis.
engineering_relevance: This enables scalable simulation of realistic microstructures under practical loads such as tension, bending, torsion, and mixed loading without requiring purely periodic models.
method: Uses a parallel distributed-memory FFT framework with discrete trigonometric transforms for Dirichlet/Neumann boundaries.
Employs a displacement-based fixed-point solver with convergence acceleration.
Builds discrete Green operators via finite-difference preconditioning for small- and finite-strain formulations.
evidence_signal: The method is evaluated on non-trivial loading scenarios, dense microstructures, and multiple constitutive laws, with comparisons between double-tetrahedron and classical hexahedral discretizations.
watch_item: Performance and convergence depend on the chosen boundary-condition symmetry, preconditioner, and discretization scheme, especially for complex nonlinear problems.
paper_title: A robust and versatile parallel FFT-based mechanical solver for general non-periodic and periodic boundary conditions
